testcase.name: test_ep_fractured_residual_to_fused_qkv_candidate[blackhole-mesh_device0-device_params0]

failure.message: KeyError: "Missing GPT-OSS layer tensor 'self_attn.q_proj.weight'; tried: model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, model.model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, model.language_model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, self_attn.q_proj.weight"

testcase.failure:
mesh_device = MeshDevice(1x4 grid, 4 devices)
@_mesh_test def test_ep_fractured_residual_to_fused_qkv_candidate(mesh_device): """Carry a real EP down partial into the next packed-QKV consumer.
The candidate pads H=2880 to 2944, uses persistent minimal RS buffers to produce a 736-wide residual shard, performs distributed RMSNorm, and consumes that shard with fused all-gather + the next layer's real packed QKV weight. This is the missing EP-to-next-attention lower-movement family; no replicated residual is restored inside the measured boundary. """
if os.environ.get("RUN_MULTICHIP_FRACTURED_BOUNDARY") != "1": pytest.skip("set RUN_MULTICHIP_FRACTURED_BOUNDARY=1 for the EP-to-QKV boundary probe") payload_name = os.environ.get("MULTICHIP_FRACTURED_PAYLOAD_DTYPE", "bfloat16") if payload_name not in ("bfloat8_b", "bfloat16"): raise ValueError("MULTICHIP_FRACTURED_PAYLOAD_DTYPE must be bfloat8_b or bfloat16") payload_dtype = {"bfloat8_b": ttnn.bfloat8_b, "bfloat16": ttnn.bfloat16}[payload_name] repeats = int(os.environ.get("MULTICHIP_FRACTURED_TRACE_REPLAYS", "20"))
config = _config() state = _real_state_dict() decoder = _decoder(state, config, mesh_device, max_cache_len=128) next_layer_idx = min(LAYER_IDX + 1, int(config.num_hidden_layers) - 1) hidden_size = int(config.hidden_size) padded_hidden_size = hidden_size + 64 local_hidden_size = padded_hidden_size // TP_DEGREE
# Recreate the actual next layer's packed QKV contract. The baseline uses # K=2880 BF16; the fractured candidate owns 64 zero K rows and uses the # API-required matched activation/weight payload dtype at K=2944. > q_weight = _require_tensor(state, next_layer_idx, "self_attn.q_proj.weight") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
models/autoports/openai_gpt_oss_20b/tests/test_multichip_decoder.py:1430: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
state_dict = {'model.layers.12.input_layernorm.weight': tensor([1.6719, 1.4844, 1.7266, ..., 1.7109, 1.9609, 1.8359], dtype..., -0.0156], [ 0.0039, -0.0156, 0.0039, ..., 0.0078, 0.0039, 0.0000]]], dtype=torch.bfloat16), ...} layer_idx = 13, suffix = 'self_attn.q_proj.weight'
def _require_tensor(state_dict: Mapping[str, object], layer_idx: int, suffix: str): value = _find_tensor(state_dict, layer_idx, suffix) if value is None: candidates = ", ".join(_candidate_keys(layer_idx, suffix)) > raise KeyError(f"Missing GPT-OSS layer tensor {suffix!r}; tried: {candidates}") E KeyError: "Missing GPT-OSS layer tensor 'self_attn.q_proj.weight'; tried: model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, model.model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, model.language_model.layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, layers.13.self_attn.q_proj.weight, self_attn.q_proj.weight"
models/autoports/openai_gpt_oss_20b/tt/functional_decoder.py:46: KeyError
